description: Averroes' Latin translations arrived at the University of Paris in the 13th century and caused an intellectual crisis. His claim that the active intellect was a single universal intellect — denying personal immortality — directly provoked Thomas Aquinas to write the Summa Theologica as a comprehensive refutation. Without Averroes, Aquinas would have written a very different book.
source: Aquinas, Summa Contra Gentiles; De Libera, A. - Averroes et l'averroïsme (2002)
